name: AppBar
purpose: Provides a top-level navigation and action bar for the current screen.
whenToUse: Providing top-level navigation (e.g., back button, drawer menu)
Housing primary screen-level actions (e.g., search, filter, profile)
whenNotToUse: Inline toolbars within specific content sections
Immersive full-screen experiences where persistent headers are distracting

accessibility.semantics: The title should implicitly or explicitly be treated as a header for screen readers.
accessibility.actions: All icon buttons in the leading and actions properties must have descriptive tooltips or semantic labels.
accessibility.contrast: Ensure sufficient contrast between the AppBar background color and the foreground text/icons.

flutterMappingNotes: Map variant foregroundColor to AppBar.foregroundColor, which automatically styles the title text and icons.
Use AppBar.iconTheme to specifically target icon colors if they differ from the foregroundColor.
Use AppBar.scrolledUnderElevation to automatically handle the 'scrolled' state in Material 3.
Do not bind Flutter shadowColor to CSS-style shadow tokens; rely on elevation and Flutter's default shadow rendering.
Use centerTitle to explicitly align the title (e.g., true for iOS-style, false for Android-style).
Set toolbarHeight if a custom height is required beyond the default Material specification.
Use systemOverlayStyle (SystemUiOverlayStyle) to ensure status bar icons contrast appropriately with the AppBar backgroundColor.
